question_text: A cyclist travels 240 metres in 30 seconds at a constant speed. Calculate the speed of the cyclist in metres per second (m/s).
explanation: Speed is calculated using the formula: speed = distance ÷ time. In this case: speed = 240 m ÷ 30 s = 8 m/s. This is a straightforward application of the speed equation where students must divide distance by time to find the constant speed.
